Caption: Half-cell potential and variation of cell potential with temperature ( d E / d T ) of (a) lithium cobalt oxide (LiCoO2), and (b) graphite from Kumaresan et al. [29].

Calculated tabulated enthalpy (cf. Eq. (38)) and entropy (cf. Eq. (39)) of intercalated lithium from the experimentally obtained half-cell E and d E / d T data shown in Fig. 1 of (a) LCO and (b) graphite.

Example of the definition of an intercalation phase on the cantera input file (CTI file) level using the new BinarySolutionTabulatedThermo class. For readability, only a small part of the tabulated data are shown (see supplementary material for full code). The CTI format for a phase declaration consists of the name of the Cantera thermodynamics class with other entries in a “name = value” format enclosed by parentheses. Required entries the phase name, a list of the constituent elements, a list of the constituent species, the definition of the tabulated_species for which the thermodynamics is varied, as well as a tabulated_thermo data for X Li [ AM ] , h Li [ AM ] 0 , tab , and s Li [ AM ] 0 , tab . The species definitions include the name, the atomic composition atoms, as well as the partial molar volume.
Modeling domain for the single-particle lithium-ion cell model. The electrolyte phase acts as an ohmic resistor. The electronically-conducting phase is represented by the dashed particle surfaces.
Simulated discharge curves starting from a fully-charged cell at different C-rates. Comparison of our model (Cantera) and Guo et al. [24].
Lithium stoichiometry within the cathode particle (upper panel) and the anode particle (lower panel) throughout the cell discharge at 1C rate.
